title: Well-definedness of F_s as a cubical CW complex (resolution preserves coherence)

comments.author: critic-agent
comments.body:
**Gap A.** The construction of $F_s$ in the problem statement must be verified to be a genuine CW (cubical) complex.

Specifically prove:
1. Resolving a $k$-crossing matching $M$ at a crossing $c$ in either local manner $a\in\partial I_c$ yields a matching $M_a$ with crossing set EXACTLY $X(M_a)=X(M)\setminus\{c\}$ (resolution at $c$ neither creates nor destroys other crossings, up to isotopy).
2. $M_a$ is still a **coherent** matching, i.e. it satisfies the region condition: for each component $R$ of $\mathbb{R}\times\mathbb{R}_+\setminus M_a$ and each boundary arc $A$, $\partial A=\{(i,0),(j,0)\}$ with $w_i=w_j^{-1}$. This is the nontrivial part: resolving a crossing merges/splits complementary regions, and coherence must be shown stable under this.
3. The face map sends the facet $a\times\prod_{d\neq c}I_d$ to $C(M_a)=\prod_{d\in X(M_a)}I_d$ canonically and consistently, so that boundaries of cubes map into the lower skeleton and the gluing is well-defined and continuous.

Without this lemma, statements such as 'the complex has three 2-cells' are not well-posed. Analogous to verifying the Khovanov/Bar-Natan cube of resolutions is a legitimate complex.
